name: Basset Hound
description: The Basset Hound was developed in France and Belgium to track hares and rabbits slowly and methodically through dense undergrowth, its low-slung body bringing its extraordinary nose within inches of the ground where scent pools and lingers longest. Despite an appearance of perpetual melancholy, Bassets are affectionate, gentle, and quietly humorous companions that get along with virtually everyone they meet. Their long, floppy ears dramatically restrict airflow to the ear canal and require weekly cleaning to prevent the chronic infections to which the breed is especially prone.

conditions_detail.name: Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD)
conditions_detail.description: IVDD occurs when the cushioning discs between vertebrae degenerate and herniate, pressing on the spinal cord. Chondrodystrophic breeds are predisposed due to premature disc mineralisation. Can cause pain, paralysis, or loss of bladder control.
symptoms: Loss of bladder or bowel control
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Mild cases are managed with strict rest and anti-inflammatory medication. Severe cases with neurological deficits require emergency spinal surgery within 24-48 hours for best outcomes.
prevention_tips: Use ramps instead of stairs for short-legged breeds

conditions_detail.name: Glaucoma
conditions_detail.description: Glaucoma is elevated intraocular pressure that damages the optic nerve and retina, causing pain and vision loss. Primary glaucoma is hereditary; secondary glaucoma results from other eye conditions. It is a veterinary emergency in acute form.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Emergency medical treatment aims to reduce intraocular pressure using topical and systemic medications. Long-term management requires ongoing medication. Affected eyes that are blind and painful are enucleated.
prevention_tips: Annual tonometry (pressure checks) in at-risk breeds
DNA test available for some breeds
Treat lens luxation and uveitis promptly to prevent secondary glaucoma
